title: joining network namespace: unknown FS magic
body:
Wow! This one has been hiding for months, perhaps longer. It seems to trigger often in int-test cleanup, where we do no error checking. I just noticed it in a real-live test failure:
```
$ podman [options] ps -q
time="2022-10-12T12:18:08Z" level=error msg="Joining network namespace for container <ID>: 
      retrieving network namespace at /run/user/6026/netns/netns-UUID:
      unknown FS magic on \"/run/user/6026/netns/netns-UUID\": 1021994"
Error: joining network namespace of container <ID>:
      retrieving network namespace at /run/user/6026/netns/netns-<UUID>:
      unknown FS magic on "/run/user/6026/netns/netns-<UUID>": 1021994
[AfterEach] Podman pod kill
```
Only one log to show right now, [ubuntu rootless](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6174187956994048/html/int-podman-ubuntu-2204-rootless-host.log.html#t--podman-pod-kill-latest-pod--1). This log file also shows three other instances of the problem, all of them non-fatal because they happen in cleanup. Search in-page for "fs magic".

comments.body: We're only storing the path in the database right now, so this conversion should not result in a breakage of DB format. We'll still blow up horribly when trying to do anything with the old namespace (e.g. start a container), but that will be a lot less often than it happening any time we try and pull a namespace out of the database. Sounds like a reasonable approach to me.

This is a thorny one, because it isn't actually triggering a test failure: the error happens in `Cleanup()`, which for complicated reasons (which I don't actually remember any more, just that they were complicated and unsolvable) cannot do exit-status checks. https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/6428ff180a3757f399b4e0fef0222c324d780eed/test/e2e/common_test.go#L537-L539

So, the only way I have to log these is if _another_ test causes a triple-fail ginkgo failure that causes a CI respin. Hence, really hard for me to monitor. Here's a list (obviously incomplete) from the last two weeks:

* Oct 20, [fedora 36 rootless](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5412605295591424/html/int-podman-fedora-36-rootless-host.log.html#t--podman-pod-clone-shm-size--1)
* Oct 21, [ubuntu 2204 rootless](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5397531035697152/html/int-podman-ubuntu-2204-rootless-host.log.html#t--podman-pod-clone-shm-size--1)
* Oct 23, [fedora 36 rootless](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5737254827065344/html/int-podman-fedora-36-rootless-host.log.html#t--podman-pod-clone-shm-size--1)

All failures are in the `podman pod clone --shm-size` e2e test, which I find interesting.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Most recent one is November 10, [f37 rootless](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/4636924405612544/html/int-podman-fedora-37-rootless-host.log.html#t--podman-pod-create-infra-inheritance-test--1) and [f36 rootless](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5762824312455168/html/int-podman-fedora-36-rootless-host.log.html#t--podman-pod-clone-shm-size--1)

Another one November 9, [f36 rootless](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6141700557504512/html/int-podman-fedora-36-rootless-host.log.html#t--podman-pod-clone-shm-size--1).

As mentioned before, this one is not trackable using my regular flake logging.
I can work on it this week. I know that there are a lot of users seeing this error as well.
This just happened to me! Is this resolved? I am on ` podman 3.4.4` via WSL2 in Windows 10
